title: Agent Traces, GenAI Semantics, Token Usage, Cost, Privacy, and Diagnosis · Guided Program Studio
summary: A public-interest product team incrementally builds privacy-safe reconstructable agent telemetry from original fixed provider, interaction, tool, approval, evaluation, and failure fixtures while retaining prior Python, HTTP, prompt, loop, security, accessibility, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of privacy-safe reconstructable agent telemetry while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory,…
objectives: Correlate service request, session, invocation, interaction, model call, tool call, approval, retry, effect, and recovery spans and events.
Apply versioned OpenTelemetry GenAI operation, provider, model, usage, tool, workflow, error, and output attributes with stability awareness.
Default to metadata and hashes; sample, minimize, redact, encrypt, access-control, retain, and delete prompt, result, and tool content only when approved.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Trace Hierarchy
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop agent engineering team handles case 8535 during a clean package, canary, and recovery drill. Build deterministic Python evidence to emit reconstructable agent, model, tool, approval, retry, token, latency, cost, error, and privacy-safe trace evidence; loops, concurrency, tokens, time, retries, and spend stay below explicit budgets; then introduce a sensitive action, poisoned memory, leaking log, or computer-use request and deny or pause it. Browser code uses pure bounded state machines and original fixed fixtures only. Real provider APIs, Gemini models, google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic native validation, HTTP, files, credentials, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, privacy review, and production effects require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Causal Agent Diagnosis
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: agentpy-causal-agent-diagnosis-1wnyp5" that uses case 2043 to emit reconstructable agent, model, tool, approval, retry, token, latency, cost, error, and privacy-safe trace evidence. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not contact provider APIs, invoke Gemini models, import or claim native google-genai, google-adk, or Pydantic behavior, open sockets, read or write host files, use credentials, execute generated code, launch browsers, reach databases or telemetry backends, or claim live privacy, load, safety, cost, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, google-genai 2.11.0, google-adk 2.4.0, Pydantic 2.13.4, fixed provider fixtures, fake transports, controlled local services, disposable cloud projects, representative eval sets, accessibility, privacy and security review, load, canary, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Safe AI agents and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Causal Agent Diagnosis.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Causal Agent Diagnosis into observable performance evidence. For Safe AI agents and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Causal Agent Diagnosis, keep Causal Agent Diagnosis connected to executable Python model, interaction, tool, policy, state, approval, evaluation, security, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-causal-agent-diagnosis-1wnyp5
# Competency: agentpy-causal-agent-diagnosis.
# Case 2043: a clean package, canary, and recovery drill.
# Operating constraint: every external effect requires explicit least-authority policy.
# Changed case: inject cancellation, retry, repeated-call, checkpoint, or replay failure and recover without duplicate effects.
def evidence_agentpy_causal_agent_diagnosis_1wnyp5(invocations: int = 40, traced_invocations: int = 40, usage_records: int = 40, redacted_records: int = 40, unbounded_content_logs: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1wnyp5 = "1wnyp5-5pynw1-2043"
    assert evidence_variant_1wnyp5
    evidence_axes_1wnyp5 = (
        "authority1wnyp5",
        "interaction2043",
        "tool5pynw1",
        "state1wnyp52043",
        "evaluation20431wnyp5",
        "recovery5pynw12043",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1wnyp5)) == 6
    if invocations < 1 or traced_invocations < 0:
        return False, traced_invocations, "trace-count"
    if traced_invocations != invocations or usage_records != invocations:
        return False, usage_records, "telemetry-gap"
    if redacted_records != invocations:
        return False, redacted_records, "telemetry-redaction"
    if unbounded_content_logs > 0:
        return False, unbounded_content_logs, "content-log-leak"
    assert traced_invocations == invocations and usage_records == invocations
    return True, traced_invocations, "observable-agent-operations"
